kept.text:
Fortinet ( $FTNT): Quiet in the AI Data Center Buildout — Primed for Physical AI Edge Security

Here’s the under-appreciated asymmetry:

1/ Right now the market is laser-focused on hyperscale build-outs. Broadcom Tomahawk 6 switches delivering 102.4 Tbps of throughput, Cisco Silicon One fabrics, 800G/1.6T interconnects, massive GPU clusters.

Fortinet sells switches too, but they lack the extreme throughput needed for these ultra-high-bandwidth AI fabrics. Their portfolio is strong for secure enterprise and campus aggregation — not for saturating the all-to-all traffic patterns inside frontier training clusters. On top of that, you don’t need a firewall on every rack or cluster. An entire AI data hall can require thousands of switches yet only a fraction as many security enforcement points.

Result: Fortinet isn’t capturing massive share in the current data-center networking surge. Fair observation.

2/ Zoom out. The real prize sits one layer deeper: physical AI.
Embodied intelligence. Humanoids and advanced robotics flooding factory floors, warehouses, and digitized manufacturing. This isn’t “more GPUs.” This is OT — operational technology — being fully subsumed by AI. Cyber-physical systems at massive scale.

3/ Here’s the critical insight most cybersecurity narratives miss:
Endpoint security will not scale here.
Traditional OT environments already prove it — non-standard OSes, real-time constraints, tiny memory/storage footprints, and extreme patch aversion make agent-based protection impractical or impossible. Adding bloat to a robotic arm or PLC is a non-starter.

Hackers understand this asymmetry perfectly. The attack surface is exploding while defensibility at the endpoint collapses.

4/ What does scale?
Network-centric security.
Micro-segmentation at wire speed. Deep packet inspection. Virtual patching for unpatchable assets. Industrial protocol awareness that actually understands what a robotic controller or PLC is supposed to be doing. Zero-trust enforcement without touching the endpoints themselves.
This is where Fortinet’s architecture shines.

5/ Their differentiator: the FortiGate 40F series — compact, fanless, desktop-sized firewalls powered by custom SP5 ASIC silicon.

Wire-speed performance in tight edge footprints. Extremely affordable at scale. Paired with FortiGuard OT intelligence (protocol decoders + virtual patching) and the Security Fabric, it creates an agentless shield purpose-built for dense, harsh, non-standard environments.

6/ Fortinet’s time to shine arrives with inference at the edge in physical AI.

Even heavily quantized models will deliver remarkable intelligence locally on humanoids and robots. This enables rich on-device reasoning with less frequent cloud round-trips — reducing latency and bandwidth dependency while increasing autonomy.

The result: millions of distributed, semi-autonomous cyber-physical nodes across smart factories worldwide. Each floor becomes its own secured micro-environment. The cumulative opportunity for compact, affordable network security solutions in this distributed world will dwarf today’s concentrated hyperscale deployments in total unit volume and footprint.

Fortinet isn’t competing on Tomahawk volumes today.
It’s quietly building the moat for the larger, messier, more defensible layer tomorrow.

Underrated setup.

Taiwan borsasının toplam piyasa değeri 4,47 trilyon dolara geldi. 

Taiwan borsasının bu olağanüstü performansının arkasındaki kilit faktör küresel yapay zeka boom'u oldu. Bu durum, başta Nvidia, Broadcom, OpenAI, Google ve Meta gibi teknoloji devlerinden gelen devasa yarı iletken talebini körükledi. 
Yerel endeksin neredeyse %45'ini oluşturan TSMC'nin piyasa değeri bu süreçte 1,8 trilyon dolara şişti.  (Bloomberg) Dünyanın en büyük çip üreticisi olan TSMC, tek başına borsanın kaderini belirliyor.
Taiwan borsası önce 16 Nisan 2026'da İngiltere'yi geçti; o tarihte toplam piyasa değeri 4,14 trilyon dolara ulaşmıştı.

kept.text:
$FN KEY READ-THROUGHS FROM FABRINET Q3 FY2026 EARNINGS CALL

Fabrinet’s Q3 FY26 call provides a high-quality cross-sector signal that AI infrastructure demand remains broad, accelerating, and increasingly constrained by components rather than end demand. The most important market read-through is that optical connectivity, DCI, 800G scale-out, accelerated compute manufacturing, and advanced photonics packaging are all moving simultaneously into larger production ramps, while supply availability in lasers, memory, and certain ASICs is now the limiting factor. This is positive for scarce component suppliers, merchant silicon vendors, coherent optics leaders, and advanced packaging ecosystems, but it also creates near-term shipment risk for downstream module, networking, and AI hardware suppliers. The call should be read less as a simple Fabrinet execution story and more as a confirmation that AI data center build-outs are stressing multiple layers of the physical infrastructure supply chain.

AI OPTICAL CONNECTIVITY, DCI AND ETHERNET NETWORKING

AI OPTICAL DEMAND IS STRONGER THAN REPORTED DATACOM REVENUE IMPLIES; COMPONENT SUPPLY IS THE LIMITING FACTOR (READ-THROUGH 1)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Coherent (COHR: US), Lumentum Holdings (LITE: US), Broadcom (AVGO: US), Marvell Technology (MRVL: US), Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI: US), Zhongji Innolight (300308: China), Eoptolink (300502: China), Accelink Technologies (002281: China).

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive for upstream optical component suppliers and merchant silicon suppliers; high magnitude for Coherent and Lumentum, medium-to-high magnitude for Broadcom and Marvell. Mixed-to-negative near term for downstream transceiver assemblers and module suppliers if they lack component allocation; medium magnitude.

Call evidence: Fabrinet reported datacom revenue of $260M, up only 4% YoY but down 6% QoQ, yet management explicitly stated that “underlying Datacom demand remains exceptionally strong” and that “demand during the quarter far exceeded what we were able to ship.” In Q&A, management added: “It’s not demand risk, it’s supply constraints,” and “Without these supply constraints, datacom revenue would have been a new record by a wide margin.” The constrained items were identified as “mainly lasers and memory” as well as “certain ASICs.”

Transmission mechanism: The call indicates that AI optical transceiver demand is not the problem; the constraint is availability of critical components in the optical module BOM. This is a direct positive for suppliers of high-speed lasers, optical components, DSPs, and ASIC content because supply scarcity supports backlog durability, pricing power, allocation leverage, and stronger demand visibility. The same fact is a near-term negative for module makers and integrators that cannot source constrained components, because reported revenue can undershoot customer demand and quarterly volatility can rise. The most important distinction is that the bottleneck appears upstream, not at the hyperscaler demand layer.

Catalyst type: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift. The near-term catalyst is Q4 supply-constrained guidance and potential upside for component suppliers reporting tight bookings. The longer-duration shift is sustained structural demand for high-speed optical components as AI networks move deeper into 800G and later 1.6T architectures.

800G SCALE-OUT IS MOVING FROM QUALIFICATION INTO PRODUCTION; THIS SUPPORTS THE AI ETHERNET SUPPLY CHAIN (READ-THROUGH 2)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Broadcom (AVGO: US), Marvell Technology (MRVL: US), Arista Networks (ANET: US), NVIDIA (NVDA: US), Coherent (COHR: US), Lumentum Holdings (LITE: US), Cisco Systems (CSCO: US).

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive; high magnitude for Broadcom and Marvell, medium-to-high magnitude for Coherent and Lumentum, medium magnitude for Arista and NVIDIA.

Call evidence: Management disclosed that Fabrinet “successfully completed qualification and have already begun shipping 2 Datacom transceiver programs directly to a hyperscale customer,” with “initial ramp starting in the fourth quarter.” Q&A clarified that both programs are “800 gig” and “scale out.” Management also stated that multiple merchant transceiver programs, “including several for data center scale-out applications,” remain on track to begin production in 2H calendar 2026, aligning with early FY27.

Transmission mechanism: 800G scale-out production directly benefits the AI Ethernet stack. Each incremental AI cluster requires high-radix switching, high-speed optical ports, PAM4/DSP content, SerDes, retimers, lasers, connectors, and transceiver assembly. Broadcom and Marvell benefit through merchant Ethernet switching silicon, high-speed connectivity silicon, and optical/DSP content. Arista benefits because the call reinforces continued hyperscaler scale-out network deployment, particularly in Ethernet-based architectures. NVIDIA benefits indirectly because high-speed scale-out connectivity is a prerequisite for larger AI cluster deployments and accelerated computing system utilization. Coherent and Lumentum benefit through high-speed optical components and lasers.

Catalyst type: Near-term trading catalyst with FY27 fundamental extension. The Q4 initial ramp is a near-term signal, but management’s expectation that volumes ramp throughout FY27 makes the larger impact a FY27 revenue and order-book event. The major caveat is that management confirmed the new hyperscaler and merchant programs use broadly similar supply chains, meaning the ramp can be capped by the same laser, memory, and ASIC constraints affecting current datacom programs.

DCI AND COHERENT OPTICS ARE ACCELERATING FASTER THAN CORE DATACOM, VALIDATING AI-DRIVEN INTER-DATA-CENTER BANDWIDTH DEMAND (READ-THROUGH 3)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Ciena (CIEN: US), Cisco Systems (CSCO: US), Coherent (COHR: US), Lumentum Holdings (LITE: US), Nokia (NOKIA: Finland), Marvell Technology (MRVL: US).

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive; high magnitude for Ciena, Coherent, and Lumentum; medium magnitude for Cisco, Nokia, and Marvell.

Call evidence: Telecom revenue was a record $628M, up 55% YoY and 13% QoQ. Within telecom, DCI revenue was $197M, up 90% YoY and 38% QoQ. Management said Fabrinet has “really all of the major players” in DCI as customers and cited 400ZR and 800ZR modules, along with telecom systems, as areas of strong participation. Management also stated that “800ZR is ramping” and that “a lot of those new programs are really in front of us.”

Transmission mechanism: AI demand is not confined to short-reach intra-data-center optics. The DCI growth rate indicates accelerating demand for coherent optical connectivity between data centers, regions, and AI capacity pools. Ciena benefits through coherent systems and DCI exposure. Cisco benefits through Acacia coherent optics and high-capacity routing/optical platforms. Coherent and Lumentum benefit through optical component and module content. Nokia benefits through optical networking exposure, particularly where data center interconnect and coherent transport are growth drivers. Marvell benefits where coherent DSP and high-speed optical silicon content participates in DCI systems.

Catalyst type: Near-term trading catalyst and longer-duration fundamental shift. The near-term catalyst is DCI growing 90% YoY and 38% QoQ, which is unusually strong for a category often viewed as cyclical. The longer-duration shift is that AI training and inference capacity increasingly require distributed data center architectures, driving sustained coherent transport demand beyond classic telco capex.

DIRECT HYPERSCALER TRANSCEIVER SOURCING IS A STRUCTURAL MARGIN RISK FOR STANDALONE MODULE VENDORS (READ-THROUGH 4)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI: US), Zhongji Innolight (300308: China), Eoptolink (300502: China), Accelink Technologies (002281: China). Positive offset for Fabrinet (FN: US), Broadcom (AVGO: US), Marvell Technology (MRVL: US), and hyperscaler procurement teams.

Directional impact and magnitude: Negative for standalone or merchant transceiver assemblers with limited differentiated component ownership; medium magnitude over a multi-quarter to multi-year horizon. Positive for neutral contract manufacturers and merchant silicon suppliers; medium magnitude.

Call evidence: Management described Fabrinet’s datacom strategy as supporting the largest customer while “actively expanding into new high-growth channels such as direct engagement with hyperscalers and partnerships with merchant vendors.” Management then disclosed 2 direct hyperscaler datacom transceiver programs. In Q&A, management emphasized that Fabrinet is a service company: “We will never have our own products. We never compete with our customers,” and Fabrinet is manufacturing “somebody else’s design.”

Transmission mechanism: Direct qualification of a neutral manufacturer by a hyperscaler shifts some value away from branded transceiver vendors and toward customer-controlled or merchant-controlled designs manufactured by scaled EMS partners. This can pressure assembly margins, reduce vendor lock-in, increase pricing transparency, and accelerate multi-sourcing. The risk is not that optical demand weakens; the risk is that more of the industry profit pool migrates toward scarce components, merchant silicon, and high-quality contract manufacturing rather than branded module assembly. Transceiver vendors with proprietary lasers, DSP integration, yield advantages, or strong hyperscaler relationships are better positioned; vendors that primarily monetize assembly are more exposed.

Catalyst type: Longer-duration fundamental shift. The near-term demand backdrop remains favorable for most high-speed optics suppliers, but the channel architecture is evolving in a way that could compress long-term gross margins for less differentiated module vendors.

TELECOM STRENGTH SHOULD NOT BE MISREAD AS A BROAD CARRIER RAN RECOVERY (READ-THROUGH 5)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Ericsson (ERIC: Sweden), Nokia (NOKIA: Finland), Ciena (CIEN: US), Cisco Systems (CSCO: US).

Directional impact and magnitude: Neutral-to-negative for RAN-centric telecom equipment expectations; low-to-medium magnitude. Positive for optical transport and DCI-exposed businesses; medium-to-high magnitude.

Call evidence: Fabrinet’s telecom revenue grew 55% YoY, but management attributed the strength heavily to DCI, including DCI revenue of $197M, up 90% YoY. The call emphasized 400ZR, 800ZR, datacenter interconnect modules, optical components, and telecom systems. There was no comparable evidence of a recovery in wireless RAN, base stations, or broad carrier access spending.

Transmission mechanism: The “telecom” label in Fabrinet’s reporting is increasingly tied to AI-driven optical transport and DCI rather than classic carrier wireless capex. This distinction matters for sector positioning. Ciena and Cisco’s optical/coherent franchises receive a clear positive signal, while Ericsson and Nokia’s RAN businesses should not receive a positive read-through from this datapoint. For Nokia, the read-through is bifurcated: positive for optical networking exposure, but not evidence of RAN recovery.

Catalyst type: Near-term sector interpretation catalyst. The call supports optical transport and DCI revisions, not broad telecom equipment multiple expansion.

AI ACCELERATED COMPUTE AND SERVER INFRASTRUCTURE

HPC MANUFACTURING DEMAND REMAINS STRONG, BUT NEXT-GENERATION TRANSITIONS CAN PUSH REVENUE TIMING BY A QUARTER (READ-THROUGH 6)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): NVIDIA (NVDA: US), Hon Hai Precision/Foxconn (2317: Taiwan), Quanta Computer (2382: Taiwan), Wiwynn (6669: Taiwan), Super Micro Computer (SMCI: US), Dell Technologies (DELL: US), Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE: US).

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive fundamental read-through, with a modest near-term timing caveat. Medium-to-high magnitude for AI server and accelerator supply-chain names; medium magnitude for NVIDIA because the call is supportive but indirect and the customer was not named.

Call evidence: Fabrinet’s HPC revenue was $107M, up 25% QoQ. Management said the current HPC program is ramping “according to our customers’ expectations,” but “not ramping in a perfect straight-line.” Management also stated that the prior $150M quarterly revenue milestone is now “pushed out by maybe 1 quarter,” while also noting that Fabrinet has been awarded “follow-on business for additional programs separate from the main programs” and is supporting the customer’s “accelerated computing infrastructure in a broader way.”

Transmission mechanism: The revenue ramp and expanded program scope confirm ongoing demand for next-generation accelerated compute infrastructure and associated manufacturing capacity. This is positive for accelerator, AI server, rack-scale integration, power/thermal, and EMS supply chains. The 1-quarter slip is not demand-negative; it reflects complexity around a new product generation transition. However, it does flag that AI infrastructure revenue recognition can be lumpy when product generations change, especially where manufacturing, qualification, and capacity installation must move together.

Catalyst type: Mixed near-term catalyst and positive longer-duration fundamental shift. The timing push can temper short-term expectations for a perfectly linear AI hardware ramp, but the broader product scope raises the implied lifetime opportunity.

AI HARDWARE GROWTH IS BECOMING MORE CAPEX- AND WORKING-CAPITAL-INTENSIVE ACROSS THE SUPPLY CHAIN (READ-THROUGH 7)

Affected Company Name (TICKER: Country): Fabrinet (FN: US), Celestica (CLS: Canada), Jabil (JBL: US), Flex (FLEX: Singapore), Sanmina (SANM: US), Super Micro Computer (SMCI: US), Dell Technologies (DELL: US), Quanta Computer (2382: Taiwan), Wiwynn (6669: Taiwan).

Directional impact and magnitude: Positive for revenue visibility; negative for free cash flow quality and balance-sheet intensity. Medium magnitude for EMS/ODM and AI hardware manufacturers.

Call evidence: Fabrinet reported Q3 operating cash flow of $53M, capex of $64M, and free cash flow outflow of $11M. Management described “continued accelerated construction of Building 10” and “capacity expansions to support the rapid growth across the business.” Building 10 adds 2M square feet and roughly $3B of revenue capacity, while additional Pinehurst and Navanakorn expansions add further capacity. Management also cited a supply-constrained environment, which generally increases the need for inventory positioning and allocation management.

Transmission mechanism: AI infrastructure suppliers are being asked to scale capacity before revenue fully materializes, while also managing constrained components. This creates a classic growth-versus-cash-conversion tension. Revenue and backlog visibility improve, but working capital, capex, and inventory risk rise. For EMS/ODM and AI hardware companies, this can create valuation dispersion: companies with superior customer prepayment terms, component allocation, inventory discipline, and high return on invested capital should outperform peers that grow revenue but dilute cash conversion.

Catalyst type: Near-term earnings quality catalyst and longer-duration fundamental issue. The near-term signal is weaker free cash flow despite strong earnings. The longer-duration issue is whether AI infrastructure growth can be converted into durable free cash flow rather than just revenue growth.

FOURTH QUARTER FORECAST

quoted_tweet.text:
WE ARE SO EARLY.

Goldman Sachs just published their CPO supply chain map for $NVDA and $AVGO. It's good but realise how many names are MISSING COMPLETLY. 

$SOI - Not listed anywhere. Goldman maps optical engines, lasers, connectors, FAUs, test equipment but completely skips the substrate layer. Every silicon photonics PIC that TSMC fabs for both NVIDIA and Broadcom's CPO platforms starts on a Soitec Photonics-SOI wafer. That's a glaring omission.

$IQE - No epiwafer layer at all. Goldman jumps from lasers to optical engines without acknowledging who grows the epitaxial layers on those laser chips.

$AIXA - No MOCVD equipment layer. Every III-V laser source listed (Sumitomo, Furukawa, VPEC, LandMark, Lumentum, Coherent) uses AIXTRON or Veeco reactors to grow their material.

$TSE - Goldman lists $TSMC as CPO chip fabrication for both ecosystems but doesn't mention Tower, which is arguably the leading independent silicon photonics foundry and is tripling capacity.

$AEHR - No wafer-level test/burn-in. Goldman lists SPIL for CPO chip test on the NVIDIA side and "TBA" for Broadcom, but misses the wafer-level burn-in step entirely.

$LPK - No glass waveguide or laser processing equipment. Goldman lists Robotechnik and ASMPT for CPO coupling but misses LPKF's LIDE technology for glass-based CPO integration.

$SIVE - Not listed under laser sources despite making InP lasers specifically designed for silicon photonics integration.

🔸CEOコメント(Alex Karp氏)

🔸会社概要
Palantir Technologies（パランティア・テクノロジーズ）は、政府・民間企業向けにAI・データ分析プラットフォームを提供するソフトウェア企業です。
主力製品であるGotham（政府向け）・Foundry（商業向け）・AIP（AI Platform）を通じて、複雑なデータの統合・分析・意思決定支援を実現しています。
AIインフラ企業として急成長中であり、米国政府・防衛領域および民間企業のデジタル変革を支援しています。
